mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, which support corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k additional jobs.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank tightening pressure, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS and HOUST both rise but SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 2%) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) creates a restrictive environment for equities, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both exceed thresholds but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST > 700k) signals strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds threshold but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) reflects broad-based economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE < 7%) indicates tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE falls below 7% but SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases.
